0) Concept

1) General form

1-1) Basic OP

1-1-0) transform 10 based interger to N based

  • to 4 base
  • to 7 base …
  • https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/doc/pic/convert_int_n_base.png
1# python
2# 504. Base 7
3
4# V0
5# IDEA : MATH : 10 based -> 7 based
6"""
7### NOTE :
8    1) for negative num : transform to positive int first, do 10 based -> 7 based op
9                          then add "-" at beginning
10    2) for positive num : do 10 based -> 7 based op
11                        -> keep checking if num % N == 0
12                        -> if not == 0, keep do  num = num % N, and append cur result to res
13                        -> reverse res
14                        -> make array to string
15                        -> return result
16    3) example:
17        100 (10 based) -> "202" (7 based)
18
19        tmp = 100
20        a, b = divmod(tmp, 7)  -> a = 14, b = 2,  tmp  = 14
21        a, b = divmod(tmp, 7)  -> a= 2, b = 0, tmp = 2
22        a, b = divmod(tmp, 7)  -> a = 0, b = 2
23
24        -> so res = [2,0,2]
25"""
26
27# V1
28class Solution(object):
29    def convertToBase7(self, num):
30        # edge case
31        if num == 0:
32            return '0'
33        tmp = abs(num)
34        res = []
35        while tmp > 0:
36            a, b = divmod(tmp, 7)
37            res.append(str(b))
38            tmp = a
39        res = res[::-1]
40        _res = "".join(res)
41        if num > 0:
42            return _res
43        else:
44            return "-" + _res
45
46# test
47#----------------
48# exmaple :
49# 7 base
50# 20 -> 26
51# -100 -> -202
52#----------------
53num = 20   # 26
54num = -100 # -202
55s = Solution()
56r = s.convertToBase7(num)
57print (r)
58
59# V1
60# https://www.itread01.com/content/1544603062.html
61# https://kknews.cc/code/jlv38qp.html
62class Solution(object):
63    def convertToBase7(self, num):
64        # edge case
65        if num == 0:
66            return '0'
67        tmp = abs(num)
68        res = []
69        while tmp:
70            i = tmp % 7
71            res.append(str(i))
72            tmp = tmp // 7
73        res = res[::-1]
74        _res = "".join(res)
75        if num > 0:
76            return _res
77        else:
78            return "-" + _res

1-1-0’) transform N based integer to 10 based

1# V1
2# LC 1022. Sum of Root To Leaf Binary Numbers
3def convertToBaseN(num, n):
4    return int(str(num), n)
5
6In [34]: int("100",7)
7Out[34]: 49
8
9In [35]: int("14",7)
10Out[35]: 11
11
12In [36]: int("66",7)
13Out[36]: 48

1-1-1) check prime number

1# LC 762 Prime Number of Set Bits in Binary Representation
2def check_prime(x):
3    if x <= 1:
4        return False
5    for i in range(2, int(x**(0.5)+1)):
6        if x % i == 0:
7            return False
8    return True

1-1-2) count prime number

1# LC 204 Count Primes
2# V0
3# IDEA : set
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/count-primes/discuss/1343795/python%3A-sieve-of-eretosthenes
5# prime(x) : number of prime in [0, x]
6# prime(0) = 0
7# prime(1) = 0
8# prime(2) = 0
9# prime(3) = 1
10# prime(4) = 2
11# prime(5) = 3
12class Solution:
13    def countPrimes(self, n):
14        # using sieve of eretosthenes algorithm
15        if n < 2: return 0
16        nonprimes = set()
17        for i in range(2, round(n**(1/2))+1):
18            if i not in nonprimes:
19                for j in range(i*i, n, i):
20                    nonprimes.add(j)
21        return n - len(nonprimes) - 2  # remove prime(1), prime(2)

1// java
2// algorithm book (labu) p.362
3// V1
4int countPrimes(int n){
5    boolean[] isPrime = new boolean[n];
6
7    // init array to true
8    Arrays.fill(isPrime, true);
9
10    // prime number start from 2
11    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++){
12        if (isPrime[i]){
13            // if i is prime, then i's multiple is NOT prime
14            for (int j = 2; j = 2 * i; j += i){
15                isPrime[j] = false;
16            }
17        }
18    }
19
20    int count = 0;
21    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++){
22        if (isPrime[i]){
23            count ++;
24        }
25    }
26    return count;
27}

1// java
2// algorithm book (labu) p.363
3// V1' (optimization)
4int countPrimes(int n){
5    boolean[] isPrime = new boolean[n];
6
7    // init array to true
8    Arrays.fill(isPrime, true);
9
10    // prime number start from 2
11    for (int i = 2; i * i  < n; i++){
12        if (isPrime[i]){
13            // if i is prime, then i's multiple is NOT prime
14            /** optimize here :  make j start from i * i, instead of 2 * i */
15            // (the only difference between V1 and V1')
16            for (int j = i * i; j < n; j += i){
17                isPrime[j] = false;
18            }
19        }
20    }
21
22    int count = 0;
23    for (int i = 2; i < n; i++){
24        if (isPrime[i]){
25            count ++;
26        }
27    }
28    return count;
29}

2) LC Example

2-1) Excel Sheet Column Title

1# 168 Excel Sheet Column Title
2# https://leetcode.com/problems/excel-sheet-column-title/discuss/205987/Python-Solution-with-explanation
3
4# V0
5# https://www.jianshu.com/p/591d3a2ab45d
6class Solution(object):
7    def convertToTitle(self, n):
8        tar = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"
9        res = ""
10        while n > 0:
11            # why (n-1) ? : idx start from 0
12            m = (n-1) % 26
13            #result += tar[m]
14            res = (tar[m] + res)
15            if m == 0:
16                # why n=n+1 ? : since there is no 0 residual (m = (n-1) % 26), so we need to "pass" this case
17                n = n + 1
18            n = (n-1) // 26
19        return res
20# V0'
21class Solution:
22    def convertToTitle(self, n):
23        """
24        :type n: int
25        :rtype: str
26        """
27        d='0ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
28        res=''
29        if n<=26:
30            return d[n]
31        else:
32            while n > 0:
33                n,r=divmod(n,26)
34                # This is the catcha on this problem where when r==0 as a result of n%26. eg, n=52//26=2, r=52%26=0. 
35                #To get 'AZ' as known for 52, n-=1 and r+=26. Same goes to 702.
36                if r == 0:
37                    n-=1
38                    r+=26
39                res = d[r] + res
40        return res

2-2) Solve the Equation

1# LC 640. Solve the Equation
2# V0
3# IDEA : replace + eval + math
4# https://leetcode.com/problems/solve-the-equation/discuss/105362/Simple-2-liner-(and-more)
5# eval : The eval() method parses the expression passed to this method and runs python expression (code) within the program.
6# -> https://www.runoob.com/python/python-func-eval.html
7class Solution(object):
8    def solveEquation(self, equation):
9        tmp = equation.replace('x', 'j').replace('=', '-(')
10        z = eval(tmp + ")" , {'j':1j})
11        # print ("equation = " + str(equation))
12        # print ("tmp = " + str(tmp))
13        # print ("z = " + str(z))
14        a, x = z.real, -z.imag
15        return 'x=%d' % (a / x) if x else 'No solution' if a else 'Infinite solutions'

2-3) Roman to Integer

1// java
2// LC 13
3
4// V0-1
5// IDEA: MAP + STR OP (fixed by gpt)
6public int romanToInt_0_1(String s) {
7    if (s == null || s.isEmpty()) {
8        return 0;
9    }
10
11    Map<Character, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
12    map.put('I', 1);
13    map.put('V', 5);
14    map.put('X', 10);
15    map.put('L', 50);
16    map.put('C', 100);
17    map.put('D', 500);
18    map.put('M', 1000);
19
20    int total = 0;
21    int prev = 0;
22
23    /**
24     * NOTE !!!
25     *
26     * loop reversely (from  idx = s.len() - 1)
27     */
28    for (int i = s.length() - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
29        int curr = map.get(s.charAt(i));
30        if (curr < prev) {
31            total -= curr;
32        } else {
33            total += curr;
34        }
35        /**
36         * NOTE !!!
37         *
38         *  set `prev` as curr
39         */
40        prev = curr;
41    }
42
43    return total;
44}
